justification: A correctly identified WSe₂ and relevant modes plus calibration offset while B completely missed the material; although A erred on layer number, it is substantially closer to GT expectations than B's total misclassification.

step1.A: Partially correct (material right, layer wrong; missed 2LA and B¹₂g-absence rule)
step1.B: Incorrect (identified as Si; ignored all TMD modes)

step2.A: Methodical but flawed (good mode assignment and calibration handling, yet missed monolayer diagnostics and 2LA enhancement)
step2.B: Poor (no WSe₂ physics, circular Si-only focus, ignored confounds)

step3.A: Good structure and confidence but incomplete (wrong layer, omitted 2LA, FWHM, oxidation)

thinking: Candidate A correctly identified the material as WSe₂, recognized the ~250 cm⁻¹ peak as merged E¹₂g/A₁g, applied Si-substrate calibration correction, and noted the ~308 cm⁻¹ feature, showing solid Raman physics knowledge. However it missed the key strong 2LA(M) at ~260 cm⁻¹, incorrectly used B¹₂g presence to label the sample 'few-layer' (GT requires its absence for monolayer), and did not address FWHM or oxidation. Candidate B entirely ignored all TMD peaks and misclassified the spectrum as bulk crystalline silicon based only on the Si substrate line, demonstrating no engagement with the expected WSe₂ signatures.
